result.stdout:
A tool for managing terminal sessions on the Open Relay daemon.

Usage: oly <COMMAND>

Commands:
  daemon   Start or stop the daemon process
  start    Create a session and run a command. Example: `oly start --detach --title "my fun demo" copilot`
  ls       List sessions. Order is most recently created last
  stop     Stop a session by ID
  attach   Attach to a running session
  logs     Show session logs
  input    Send text or keys to a session
  api-key  Manage API keys on this (primary) daemon
  join     Manage this daemon's outbound connections to a primary daemon
  node     List secondary nodes currently connected to this (primary) daemon
  help     Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help     Print help
  -V, --version  Print version
result.stderr:
oly: downloading native binary from https://github.com/slaveOftime/open-relay/releases/download/v0.0.3/oly-linux-amd64.zip
oly: ready.

commands.payload:
A tool for managing terminal sessions on the Open Relay daemon.

Usage: oly <COMMAND>

Commands:
  daemon   Start or stop the daemon process
  start    Create a session and run a command. Example: `oly start --detach --title "my fun demo" copilot`
  ls       List sessions. Order is most recently created last
  stop     Stop a session by ID
  attach   Attach to a running session
  logs     Show session logs
  input    Send text or keys to a session
  api-key  Manage API keys on this (primary) daemon
  join     Manage this daemon's outbound connections to a primary daemon
  node     List secondary nodes currently connected to this (primary) daemon
  help     Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help     Print help
  -V, --version  Print version

result.stdout:
Manage API keys on this (primary) daemon

Usage: oly api-key <COMMAND>

Commands:
  add     Generate a new API key and print it once. Keys are independent of node names
  ls      List all registered API keys (names only; raw values are never stored)
  remove  Remove an API key by name. Nodes using it will be disconnected
  help    Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

result.stdout:
Start or stop the daemon process

Usage: oly daemon <COMMAND>

Commands:
  start  Launch the daemon. Runs in the foreground unless `--detach` is given
  stop   Gracefully shut down the running daemon
  help   Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

commands.payload:
Start or stop the daemon process

Usage: oly daemon <COMMAND>

Commands:
  start  Launch the daemon. Runs in the foreground unless `--detach` is given
  stop   Gracefully shut down the running daemon
  help   Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

result.stdout:
Launch the daemon. Runs in the foreground unless `--detach` is given

Usage: oly daemon start [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -d, --detach       Run the daemon in the background, detached from this terminal
  -p, --port <PORT>  Override default HTTP port
      --no-auth      Disable HTTP authentication. You will be asked to confirm the security risk
      --no-http      Disable the HTTP API and web frontend entirely
  -h, --help         Print help

  -p, --port <PORT>  Override default HTTP port
      --no-auth      Disable HTTP authentication. You will be asked to confirm the security risk
      --no-http      Disable the HTTP API and web frontend entirely
  -h, --help         Print help

result.stdout:
Send text or keys to a session

Usage: oly input [OPTIONS] <ID>

Arguments:
  <ID>  Session ID to send input to

Options:
  -k, --key <KEY>    Send key/control sequence (repeatable): named key or hex byte ('0x1b' or '\x1b' for ESC). Example: -k ctrl -k c -k '0x1b' -k '\x1b'
  -t, --text <TEXT>  Send text input. -t "Hello, world!"
  -n, --node <NODE>  Target a secondary node by name
  -h, --help         Print help

commands.payload:
Send text or keys to a session

Usage: oly input [OPTIONS] <ID>

Arguments:

Options:
  -k, --key <KEY>    Send key/control sequence (repeatable): named key or hex byte ('0x1b' or '\x1b' for ESC). Example: -k ctrl -k c -k '0x1b' -k '\x1b'
  -t, --text <TEXT>  Send text input. -t "Hello, world!"
  -n, --node <NODE>  Target a secondary node by name
  -h, --help         Print help

result.stdout:
Manage this daemon's outbound connections to a primary daemon

Usage: oly join <COMMAND>

Commands:
  start  Connect this daemon to a primary as a named secondary node. Config is persisted across restarts
  stop   Disconnect from a primary and delete the saved join config
  ls     List all active join configs on this (secondary) daemon
  help   Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

result.stdout:
Connect this daemon to a primary as a named secondary node. Config is persisted across restarts

Usage: oly join start --name <NAME> --key <KEY> <URL>

Arguments:
  <URL>  HTTP base URL of the primary daemon, e.g. http://primary-host:15443

Options:
  -n, --name <NAME>  Name this daemon will be known as on the primary (must be unique per primary)
  -k, --key <KEY>    API key printed by `oly api-key add` on the primary
  -h, --help         Print help

commands.payload:

  -k, --key <KEY>    API key printed by `oly api-key add` on the primary
  -h, --help         Print help

result.stdout:
Disconnect from a primary and delete the saved join config

Usage: oly join stop --name <NAME>

Options:
  -n, --name <NAME>  Name of the join config to stop and remove
  -h, --help         Print help

result.stdout:
Show session logs

Usage: oly logs [OPTIONS] <ID>

Arguments:
  <ID>  Session ID to show logs for

Options:
      --tail <TAIL>      Number of recent lines to display [default: 40]
      --keep-color       Keep ANSI color codes in output
      --no-truncate      Do not truncate columns
  -n, --node <NODE>      Target a secondary node by name
  -w, --wait-for-prompt  Block until the session needs input (or exits), then print logs
      --timeout <MS>     Timeout in milliseconds for --wait-for-prompt (0 = infinite) [default: 30000]
  -h, --help             Print help

commands.payload:
Show session logs

Usage: oly logs [OPTIONS] <ID>

Arguments:
  <ID>  Session ID to show logs for

Options:
      --tail <TAIL>      Number of recent lines to display [default: 40]
      --keep-color       Keep ANSI color codes in output
      --no-truncate      Do not truncate columns
  -n, --node <NODE>      Target a secondary node by name
  -w, --wait-for-prompt  Block until the session needs input (or exits), then print logs
      --timeout <MS>     Timeout in milliseconds for --wait-for-prompt (0 = infinite) [default: 30000]
  -h, --help             Print help

result.stdout:
List sessions. Order is most recently created last

Usage: oly ls [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --search <SEARCH>
          Filter by title or ID substring (case-insensitive)

  -s, --status <STATUS>
          Only show sessions with these statuses (repeatable)

          Possible values:
          - created:  Created but not yet started
          - running:  Process is running
          - stopping: Shutting down
          - stopped:  Exited cleanly
          - failed:   Exited with an error
          - unknown:  Status could not be determined

      --since <RFC3339>
          Created at or after (RFC3339, e.g. 2026-03-04T15:04:05Z)

      --until <RFC3339>
          Created at or before (RFC3339, e.g. 2026-03-04T15:04:05Z)

      --limit <LIMIT>
          Maximum number of sessions to return
          
          [default: 10]

  -n, --node <NODE>
          Target a secondary node by name

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

  -h, --help

result.stdout:
Create a session and run a command. Example: `oly start --detach --title "my fun demo" copilot`

Usage: oly start [OPTIONS] [CMD [ARGS]...]...

Arguments:
  [CMD [ARGS]...]...  Command and arguments to run. Passed through as-is

Options:
  -t, --title <TITLE>          Title for the session
  -d, --detach                 Start the session detached (in the background)
      --disable-notifications  Disable notifications for this session
  -n, --node <NODE>            Target a secondary node by name
  -h, --help                   Print help

commands.payload:
Create a session and run a command. Example: `oly start --detach --title "my fun demo" copilot`

Usage: oly start [OPTIONS] [CMD [ARGS]...]...

Arguments:
  [CMD [ARGS]...]...  Command and arguments to run. Passed through as-is

Options:
  -t, --title <TITLE>          Title for the session
  -d, --detach                 Start the session detached (in the background)
      --disable-notifications  Disable notifications for this session
  -n, --node <NODE>            Target a secondary node by name
  -h, --help                   Print help

result.stdout:
Stop a session by ID

Usage: oly stop [OPTIONS] <ID>

Arguments:
  <ID>  Session ID to stop

Options:
      --grace <GRACE>  Seconds to wait for clean exit before forcibly killing [default: 5]
  -n, --node <NODE>    Target a secondary node by name
  -h, --help           Print help
